01-01.latin: Deus, qui salútis ætérnæ, beátæ Maríæ virginitáte fecúnda, humáno géneri prǽmia præstitísti: tríbue, quǽsumus; ut ipsam pro nobis intercédere sentiámus, per quam merúimus auctórem vitæ suscípere, Dóminum nostrum Jesum Christum Fílium tuum.
01-01.english: O God, who by the fruitful virginity of blessed Mary, hast given to mankind the rewards of eternal salvation: grant, we beseech Thee, that we may experience her intercession for us, through whom we have been made worthy to receive the Author of life, Our Lord Jesus Christ Thy Son.

01-06.latin: Deus, qui hodiérna die Unigénitum tuum géntibus stella duce revelásti: concéde propítius; ut, qui jam te ex fide cognóvimus, usque ad contemplándam spéciem tuæ celsitúdinis perducámur.
01-06.english: O God, who on this day didst reveal Thine only-begotten Son to the Gentiles by the leading of a star: mercifully grant that we, who know Thee now by faith, may be brought to contemplate the beauty of Thy majesty.

02-02.latin: Omnípotens sempitérne Deus, majestátem tuam súpplices exorámus: ut, sicut Unigénitus Fílius tuus hodiérna die cum nostræ carnis substántia in templo est præsentátus; ita nos fácias purificátis tibi méntibus præsentári.
02-02.english: Almighty and everlasting God, we humbly beseech Thy Majesty, that, as Thine only-begotten Son was this day presented in the temple in the substance of our flesh, so may we be presented unto Thee with purified minds.

03-19.latin: Sanctíssimæ Genetrícis tuæ Sponsi, quǽsumus, Dómine, méritis adjuvémur: ut, quod possibílitas nostra non óbtinet, ejus nobis intercessióne donétur.
03-19.english: We beseech Thee, O Lord, that we may be helped by the merits of the Spouse of Thy most holy Mother: so that what we cannot obtain of ourselves, may be given us through his intercession.

03-25.latin: Deus, qui de beátæ Maríæ Vírginis útero Verbum tuum, Angelo nuntiánte, carnem suscípere voluísti: præsta supplícibus tuis; ut, qui vere eam Genetrícem Dei crédimus, ejus apud te intercessiónibus adjuvémur.
03-25.english: O God, who didst will that Thy Word should take flesh, at the message of an Angel, in the womb of the Blessed Virgin Mary: grant to us, Thy humble servants, that, as we believe her to be truly the Mother of God, we may be helped by her intercession with Thee.

08-15.latin: Omnípotens sempitérne Deus, qui Immaculátam Vírginem Maríam, Fílii tui Genetrícem, córpore et ánima ad cæléstem glóriam assumpsísti: concéde, quǽsumus; ut, ad supérna semper inténti, ipsíus glóriæ mereámur esse consórtes.
08-15.english: Almighty everlasting God, who hast taken body and soul into heaven the Immaculate Virgin Mary, Mother of Thy Son: grant, we beseech Thee, that, ever intent upon heavenly things, we may be found worthy to be partakers of her glory.

09-29.latin: Deus, qui miro órdine Angelórum ministéria hominúmque dispénsas: concéde propítius; ut, a quibus tibi ministrántibus in cælo semper assístitur, ab his in terra vita nostra muniátur.
09-29.english: O God, who dost arrange the services of Angels and men in a wonderful order: mercifully grant that our life may be protected on earth by those who always serve Thee in heaven.

11-01.latin: Omnípotens sempitérne Deus, qui nos ómnium Sanctórum tuórum mérita sub una tribuísti celebritáte venerári: quǽsumus; ut desiderátam nobis tuæ propitiatiónis abundántiam, multiplicátis intercessóribus, largiáris.
11-01.english: Almighty and everlasting God, who hast granted us to honor the merits of all Thy Saints in one solemnity: bestow upon us, we beseech Thee, through so many intercessors, an abundance of the reconciliation for which we long.

12-08.latin: Deus, qui per Immaculátam Vírginis Conceptiónem dignum Fílio tuo habitáculum præparásti: quǽsumus; ut, qui ex morte ejúsdem Fílii tui prævísa eam ab omni labe præservásti, nos quoque mundos ejus intercessióne ad te perveníre concédas.
12-08.english: O God, who by the Immaculate Conception of the Virgin, didst prepare a worthy habitation for Thy Son: we beseech Thee, that, as by the foreseen death of that same Son, Thou didst preserve her from all stain, so too Thou wouldst permit us, purified through her intercession, to come unto Thee.

12-25.latin: Concéde, quǽsumus, omnípotens Deus: ut nos Unigéniti tui nova per carnem Natívitas líberet, quos sub peccáti jugo vetústa sérvitus tenet.
12-25.english: Grant, we beseech Thee, almighty God, that the new birth of Thine only-begotten Son in the flesh may set us free, who are held by the old bondage under the yoke of sin.

_default.latin: Deus, qui nos in tantis perículis constitútos, pro humána scis fragilitáte non posse subsístere: da nobis salútem mentis et córporis; ut ea, quæ pro peccátis nostris pátimur, te adjuvánte vincámus.
_default.english: O God, who knowest that we are set amidst such great dangers, that by reason of the frailty of our nature we cannot stand: grant us health of mind and body, that the things which we suffer for our sins, we may overcome by Thy help.
